name: King Sejong
description: King Sejong the Great (1397-1450) was the fourth monarch of the Joseon Dynasty of Korea. He is revered as one of the greatest rulers in Korean history, celebrated for his profound contributions to Korean culture, science, and governance. Born Yi Do, he ascended the throne in 1418 and immediately set about reforming the kingdom. His most significant achievement was the creation of Hangul, the Korean alphabet, in 1443. Prior to Hangul, Koreans primarily used Classical Chinese for writing, which was inaccessible to the majority of the population. Sejong recognized the need for a writing system that was both easy to learn and reflective of the Korean language. With the help of scholars in the Hall of Worthies (Jiphyeonjeon), he developed Hangul, a feat that democratized literacy and fostered a unique Korean cultural identity. Beyond Hangul, Sejong oversaw advancements in agriculture, astronomy, medicine, and military technology. He promoted Confucianism, implemented legal reforms, and strengthened national defense. His reign marked a golden age of Korean history, laying the foundation for a more prosperous and culturally rich society. Sejong's legacy continues to inspire Koreans today, serving as a symbol of enlightened leadership and national pride. His image is featured on the Korean 10,000 won bill, a testament to his enduring impact on the nation.
subtitle: Creator of Hangul, the Korean Alphabet

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Hangul
keyMyths.description: King Sejong, deeply concerned about the illiteracy of his people, secretly commissioned the creation of a new, simple alphabet. He faced opposition from conservative officials who favored the established Chinese writing system, but he persevered and successfully introduced Hangul to the nation.

keyMyths.title: Sejong's Health
keyMyths.description: It is said that King Sejong suffered from various ailments throughout his life, including diabetes and eye problems. Despite his failing health, he remained dedicated to his duties and continued to work tirelessly for the betterment of his kingdom, often neglecting his own well-being.

keyMyths.title: The Water Clock
keyMyths.description: King Sejong encouraged the creation of automated water clocks, known as Jagyeongnu, to accurately measure time. These clocks were a significant technological advancement and helped to regulate the daily lives of the people and improve administrative efficiency.

sources.author: Park, Hyun-moo
sources.description: A comprehensive biography of King Sejong, covering his life, reign, and achievements.
sources.text: A comprehensive biography of King Sejong, covering his life, reign, and achievements.
